heine1834.description: Königsberg townspeople are said to have set their clocks by his walk time.
heine1834.mechanism: Community-level temporal anchor; ritualised predictability reinforces the agent's own schedule compliance. Flagged as legend by ERRE-Sandbox v0.2 §3.1.

jachmann1804.description: Midday dinner 13:00-16:00 with 4-9 invited guests, topic-driven conversation ("minimum Graces, maximum Muses").
jachmann1804.mechanism: Social cognition plus postprandial discourse; the 4-9 cap preserves a small-group discussion regime that sustains deep argumentation.

kuehn2001.description: Daily walk on the Linden-Allee at 15:30 (±5 min) for 60-75 minutes; no conversation on the return leg.
kuehn2001.mechanism: Rhythmic locomotion activates the Default Mode Network; Oppezzo & Schwartz (2014) report a +60% gain on Guilford's Alternate Uses divergent-thinking task (treadmill and outdoor-loop conditions); the outdoor effect size for sustained 60-minute walks is not directly measured in that study.

kuehn2001.description: Nasal-only breathing during walks, deliberately suppressing speech.
kuehn2001.mechanism: Kant advocates nasal breathing in Anthropologie §44 as generally beneficial, but that his personal practice on walks was nasal-only is biographical tradition (Kuehn 2001 ch. 8) rather than a direct self-report. Hypothesised vagal-tone modulation via nasal respiration remains speculative.

kuehn2001.description: Morning writing window 05:00-07:00 devoted to long-form critique drafting.
kuehn2001.mechanism: Chronotype-aligned prefrontal window supports multi-step deductive reasoning; early-morning low-interference improves working memory.

kuehn2001.description: Never travelled more than ~10 miles from Königsberg in his entire life.
kuehn2001.mechanism: Local embedded spatial memory may scaffold abstract conceptual navigation (grid-code-on-concept hypothesis, Constantinescu et al. 2016). The original study does not address travel range or philosophical productivity; this linkage is our extrapolation. The behavioural fact (never >10 miles) is well-attested; the cognitive consequence is speculative.

citation.author: Heinrich Heine
citation.notes: Cited by the 'Koenigsberg townspeople set their clocks by his walk' legend habit. Work is PD by age, but no exact PD edition + retrieval URL has been confirmed, so body_committable stays false and the body is not committed in this MVP.
citation.pd_basis: author Heine d.1856; work first published 1834 — PD by age
citation.title: Zur Geschichte der Religion und Philosophie in Deutschland

citation.author: Reinhold Bernhard Jachmann
citation.notes: Cited by the midday-dinner (4-9 guests) habit. Work is PD by age, but no exact PD edition + retrieval URL has been confirmed, so body_committable stays false and the body is not committed in this MVP.
citation.pd_basis: author Jachmann d.1843; work published 1804 — PD by age
citation.title: Immanuel Kant geschildert in Briefen an einen Freund

citation.author: Manfred Kuehn
citation.notes: Cited by 4 kant cognitive_habits (daily walk, nasal-only breathing, morning writing window, never-travelled->10-miles). Raw body NOT committed: modern in-copyright biography. Cannot substantiate the habit text beyond the citation; confirm citation before relying on it.
citation.title: Kant: A Biography
